steps.title: Identify the list structure
steps.content: The sentence lists three deserts. Each desert is named and then immediately followed by a comma and its ranking.

steps.title: Apply the punctuation rule
steps.content:
Three deserts are listed; each has a ranking description with a comma inside it. When list items contain internal commas, semicolons separate the items.
  • Choice A — misplaced semicolon: "ninth in size, the Kyzylkum Desert; ranked sixteenth," — the semicolon lands between "Desert" and "ranked," splitting the Kyzylkum entry in two instead of separating it from the Syrian Desert entry. ✗
  • Choice B — semicolons between items, commas within: "ninth in size; the Kyzylkum Desert, ranked sixteenth;" — semicolons divide the three desert entries; the comma inside "Kyzylkum Desert, ranked sixteenth" is correct. ✓
  • Choice C — no item separator: "ninth in size the Kyzylkum Desert, ranked sixteenth" — no semicolon separates the Syrian Desert entry from the Kyzylkum entry, creating a run-on list. ✗
  • Choice D — semicolon inside an item: "ninth in size; the Kyzylkum Desert; ranked sixteenth;" — the second semicolon splits "the Kyzylkum Desert" from its own ranking description. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
